Fix: clean stale .agentsquad task lock files on runner startup

On each runner startup, CleanupStaleTaskFilesAsync scans the repo's
default branch for .agentsquad/*.task and .agentsquad/*.tracking files
and deletes them. This prevents stale locks from previous runs from
confusing fresh runs with phantom in-progress tasks.

General improvements: built-in defaults, config-file report options, doc restructuring (#155)

* Initial plan

* feat: add built-in default configuration with Dependency Updates section

When no .buildmark.yaml file is present, BuildMark now applies a default
configuration with three report sections (Changes, Bugs Fixed, Dependency
Updates) and six routing rules that classify items by label and work-item
type. This ensures dependency-manager PRs from Renovate/Dependabot are
automatically routed to the Dependency Updates section out of the box.

Co-authored-by: Malcolmnixon <1863707+Malcolmnixon@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add match condition assertions to default config test

Verify section titles, label match lists, work-item-type match lists,
and the catch-all rule's null match for complete coverage of the
built-in default configuration.

Co-authored-by: Malcolmnixon <1863707+Malcolmnixon@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: allow report options to be set in config file with CLI override

Add ReportConfig record for report generation options (file, depth,
include-known-issues) and integrate it into BuildMarkConfig.

Parse the new 'report' section in BuildMarkConfigReader following the
same pattern as the existing connector parser.

Update Program.ProcessBuildNotes to resolve effective report options
where CLI arguments override config file values.

Add tests for valid report section parsing and invalid depth validation.

fix: zero active_model_calls when session not resumed (#908)

When only ASSISTANT_TURN_START events follow the final shutdown (no
USER_MESSAGE, ASSISTANT_MESSAGE, or SESSION_RESUME), _detect_resume
correctly sets session_resumed=False but _build_completed_summary
still propagated the orphaned post_shutdown_turn_starts into
active_model_calls. This caused has_active_period_stats() to return
True, leading _compute_session_totals(shutdown_only=True) to
subtract phantom active calls from the displayed totals.

Fix: set active_model_calls to 0 when resume.session_resumed is
False, eliminating the inconsistency at the source.

* Fix SSRF: use host IP for VK -> interLink API connection

The Virtual Kubelet binary has an SSRF guard that blocks requests to
localhost.  With InterlinkURL set to "http://localhost" the VK could
never POST to the interLink API (port 3000) - every create/status/delete
call returned "potential SSRF detected: http://localhost:3000/...".

Switch InterlinkURL in vk-config.yaml to use ${POD_IP} (the real host
IP already computed above) so the VK reaches the API via a non-loopback
address that passes the SSRF guard.

The vk-test-set pods request CPUs using Kubernetes millicores notation
(e.g. "100m"), which caused a ValueError when the plugin tried to cast
the string directly to int().

Add parse_cpu() which handles all Kubernetes CPU formats:
  - millicores: "100m" -> 1, "1500m" -> 2
  - plain int/float: "1", "2", "0.5" -> 1, 1, 1 (ceil, min 1)

Use parse_cpu() at both call sites (produce_htcondor_singularity_script
and the remote-condor JDL path).  Also add "import math" for math.ceil.

* handles.py: fix mount_empty_dir AttributeError on dict volumes

The e2e tests were failing with:
  AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'name'
at handles.py line 333 in mount_empty_dir.

The function was using Python attribute access (vol.name, pod.namespace,
pod.uid) on plain dicts received from the interLink JSON payload, and also
referenced non-existent keys (vol["volumeSource"] → no such key in K8s
volume dicts; pod_volume_spec["EmptyDir"] → wrong capitalisation and wrong
field; mount_spec["mount_path"] → should be mount_spec["mountPath"]).

Fixes:
  vol.name              → vol["name"]         (x2)
  vol["volumeSource"]   → vol               (vol IS the volume dict)
  pod_volume_spec["EmptyDir"] → "emptyDir" in pod_volume_spec
  pod.namespace         → pod["metadata"]["namespace"]
  pod.uid               → pod["metadata"]["uid"]
  mount_spec["mount_path"] → mount_spec["mountPath"]

* handles.py: fix IndexError in mount loop caused by trailing comma in ed_path

mount_empty_dir was appending a trailing "," to ed_path before returning it.
When prepare_mounts called ",".join(mount_data), the already-comma-terminated
emptyDir entry produced double commas, e.g.:
  /data/ns/emptyDirs/vol:/mountPath/vol,,

After split(","), this yields ["…/vol", "/mountPath/vol", "", ""] — fragments
without ":" — causing mount.split(":")[1] at SubmitHandler line 915 to raise
IndexError: list index out of range.

Two fixes:
1. mount_empty_dir: strip the trailing "," from ed_path, matching the format
   returned by mountConfigMaps/mountSecrets (plain "host:container" strings).
2. SubmitHandler loop: add a defensive guard to skip empty or non-":" entries,
   making the loop robust against any future malformed mount strings.
